label: A — London, British Library, MS Cotton Tiberius B.viii
shelfmark: London, British Library, MS Cotton Tiberius B.viii
description: The Coronation Book (Livre du sacre) of Charles V. Over 40 miniatures. Signed by the king on fol. 74v. Taken to England by Duke of Bedford after 1427. Incomplete: missing leaves between fols. 48v–9r and 53v–4r. Base text.
bibliography: Dewick, Coronation Book, xi–xii; Sherman, 'The Queen in Charles V's Coronation Book' (1977)

label: B — Paris, BnF, MS lat. 8886
origin: Bayeux (pontifical) / for Jean, Duke of Berry
description: Magnificent pontifical. Given to Sainte-Chapelle in Bourges 1405–7. Not copied from A.

label: C — Paris, BnF, MS lat. 9479
description: Queen's ordo only (nos. 87–08). Copied from A.

label: D — London, British Library, MS Additional 32097
F — Paris, Archives de Saint-Sulpice, MS 1928
description: Copied from livre rouge (lost MS 4). Later collated with livre rouge, with marginal additions. Important witness to lost MS.

label: G — Paris, BnF, MS lat. 13314
description: Copied from livre rouge. King's ordo only. Best copy of the three witnesses to the livre rouge.
